items.name: Physical Memory Dump
items.description: Full RAM capture — running processes, decrypted data, injected code, connection table, loaded drivers. Collect first; everything else is on disk.

items.name: Running Processes
items.description: Snapshot of active processes with PIDs, parents, command lines, and loaded modules.

items.name: Network Connections
items.description: Active TCP/UDP connections, listening sockets, and the owning process — critical for catching live C2.

items.name: Logged-in Users
items.description: Interactive and RDP sessions currently active on the host.

items.name: ARP Cache & Routing Table
items.description: Layer-2 neighbours and kernel routing table — reveals VPN tunnels and stale entries.

items.name: SYSTEM hive
items.description: Service configuration, device drivers, mounted devices, timezone, USB history, ShimCache.

items.name: SOFTWARE hive
items.description: Installed applications, uninstall keys, Run/RunOnce persistence, default user settings.

items.name: SECURITY hive
items.description: Local security policy, cached domain credentials (LSA secrets).

items.name: SAM hive
items.description: Local user accounts and password hashes. Pair with SYSTEM to decrypt.

items.name: NTUSER.DAT (each user)
items.description: Per-user registry: UserAssist, TypedURLs, RecentDocs, Run keys, shell bags.

items.name: UsrClass.dat (each user)
items.description: Per-user shell bags, recent Explorer activity, opened folder history.

items.name: Amcache.hve
items.description: Executed program inventory with SHA-1 hashes, first-run times, and binary metadata. Amcache replaced RecentFileCache on Win 8+.

items.name: Security.evtx
items.description: Logons (4624/4625), privilege use, account changes, Kerberos auth.

items.name: System.evtx
items.description: Service starts/stops, driver loads, system boot/shutdown.

items.name: Application.evtx
items.description: Application crashes, errors, and informational events from installed software.

items.name: PowerShell Operational
items.description: Script-block logging (4104), module loads, pipeline execution — if enabled.

items.name: Task Scheduler Operational
items.description: Scheduled-task registration, triggers, and runs (persistence).

items.name: Sysmon Operational (if deployed)
items.description: Process creation with hashes, network connects, file creates, registry changes — only present if Sysmon is installed.

items.name: Defender Operational
items.description: AV scan results, detections, real-time protection events. Defender is built-in from Win 8 / Server 2012 onward; Win 7 had a reduced anti-spyware variant without this log.

items.name: RDP Local Session Manager
items.description: Inbound RDP session start / disconnect / reconnect events with usernames and source IPs.

items.name: RDP Remote Connection Manager
items.description: Earlier-stage RDP events — connection requests and authentication steps. Complements LocalSessionManager.

items.name: AD Directory Service log
items.description: Classic AD DS event log — schema changes, DB errors, replication issues, KDC events. Only on Domain Controllers.

items.name: DNS Server log
items.description: DNS server events. Enable the separate DNS analytic/debug log at the DNS console for per-query logging.

items.name: DFS Replication log
items.description: SYSVOL replication events (DFSR replaced FRS). Failures here mean GPO/scripts may be stale on some DCs.

items.name: Netlogon Operational log
items.description: Netlogon service events — secure-channel negotiation, trust queries. Surges of 5723/5719 suggest broken trusts or lateral movement.

items.name: AD DS Operational log
items.description: Modern AD DS operational log — detailed replication, schema, and service events. Present on Server 2012 onwards.

items.name: Kerberos Operational log
items.description: Kerberos client/server events. Often empty unless Kerberos logging is explicitly enabled, but worth grabbing in case it was.

items.name: Prefetch (*.pf)
items.description: Records the last 8 executions per binary — name, run count, last-run timestamps, referenced files. Disabled by default on Windows Server.

items.name: ShimCache (AppCompatCache)
items.description: File path, size, and last-modified time of executed binaries. Lives inside the SYSTEM hive — collect that instead.

items.name: BAM / DAM
items.description: Background Activity Moderator — per-user last-run times for executed binaries. In the SYSTEM hive.

items.name: SRUM database
items.description: System Resource Usage Monitor — per-app network bytes, CPU/energy usage, historical.

items.name: LNK files (Recent)
items.description: Shortcut files auto-created when a user opens a file — target path, target size, MAC times, volume serial.

items.name: Jump Lists
items.description: Per-application recent-file lists (Office, Explorer, etc.) — richer than LNKs for app-specific activity.

items.name: Chrome profile
items.description: History, Cookies, Login Data (SQLite), Bookmarks, Top Sites, Shortcuts, Cache.

items.name: Edge (Chromium) profile
items.description: Same structure as Chrome — History, Cookies, Login Data, Cache.

items.name: Firefox profile
items.description: places.sqlite (history + bookmarks), cookies.sqlite, formhistory, logins.json.

items.name: PowerShell history (ConsoleHost_history.txt)
items.description: Plain-text log of every interactive PowerShell command per user — survives reboots. PSReadLine is built-in from Win 10 / Server 2016 onward.

items.name: Screenshots folder
items.description: Win+PrtScn auto-saves numbered PNGs here (feature added in Win 8). Also check OneDrive's screenshot redirect if the user enabled it.

items.name: Windows Timeline (ActivitiesCache.db)
items.description: SQLite DB of recent app activity and opened files (Win 10 1803+). Client OS only — removed from Server defaults.

items.name: Thumbnail cache
items.description: Per-user cached thumbnails of previously-viewed images and documents — recoverable even after the originals are deleted.

items.name: RDP bitmap cache
items.description: Client-side tile cache of outbound RDP sessions — can be reassembled to show fragments of what the user viewed remotely.

items.name: Office Recent files
items.description: Shortcuts to documents opened in Office — complements the generic Recent LNK list with app-attributable data.

items.name: OneDrive / cloud sync folders
items.path: C:\Users\<user>\OneDrive\ (and vendor variants for Dropbox, Google Drive, iCloud, Teams)
items.description: Cloud-synced files often hold data deleted from the local disk. Also grab the client's sync logs (e.g. %LocalAppData%\Microsoft\OneDrive\logs\).

items.name: $MFT (Master File Table)
items.description: Every file and directory entry on the volume with timestamps, sizes, and resident data. Use a raw-disk tool to extract.

items.name: $UsnJrnl:$J
items.description: Filesystem change journal — file creates/renames/deletes with timestamps. Usually goes back days to weeks.

items.name: pagefile.sys
items.description: Swap. Can contain fragments of process memory including command lines and strings.

items.name: hiberfil.sys
items.description: Hibernation file — compressed snapshot of RAM at last sleep. Gold for offline analysis.

items.name: swapfile.sys
items.description: Modern-app paging file (Win 8+). Smaller than pagefile but still yields memory fragments.

items.name: Recycle Bin ($Recycle.Bin)
items.description: $I* files hold original path, delete time, and size; $R* files hold the actual deleted content. One subfolder per user SID.

items.name: Volume Shadow Copies
items.description: Historical snapshots of the entire volume — often weeks of restorable state. List with vssadmin, then mount each snapshot with mklink to browse offline.

items.name: Windows Search index
items.description: ESE database with a full-text index of indexed locations (docs, email) — can yield content of files that have been deleted.

items.name: Run / RunOnce registry keys
items.description: Most-common persistence mechanism — included when you save SOFTWARE + NTUSER.DAT.

items.name: Startup folders
items.description: Drops here auto-run at logon. Also check the All Users equivalent under ProgramData.

items.name: Scheduled Tasks folder
items.description: XML definitions of every scheduled task — full command line, principal, triggers.

items.name: Services registry
items.description: Full service definitions including ImagePath (captured when you save SYSTEM).

items.name: WMI Repository
items.description: WMI event subscriptions are a stealth persistence vector — this repository holds them.

items.name: setupapi.dev.log
items.description: Plug-and-Play device install log — first-connect timestamps for every USB stick and hardware device, regardless of current presence. Pair with USBSTOR / USB registry keys (already captured via the SYSTEM hive) for full device provenance.

items.name: ntds.dit (AD database)
items.description: The AD database — every user, group, computer, and password hash in the domain. ESE format; locked on a running DC, use ntdsutil ifm or a VSS snapshot to grab it safely.

items.name: AD transaction + reserve logs
items.description: ESE transaction and reserve log files. Needed alongside ntds.dit to replay uncommitted changes when opening the DB offline.

items.name: SYSVOL (GPO + scripts)
items.description: Replicated share holding Group Policy Objects, logon scripts, and anything pushed via GPO. Common persistence drop-spot for domain-wide payloads.

items.name: GPO definitions
items.description: One folder per GPO (GUID-named). Contains Registry.pol, startup/shutdown scripts, scheduled-task XMLs, and Group Policy Preferences — a rich persistence surface.

items.name: DNS zone files (if file-backed)
items.description: DNS zone data for non-AD-integrated zones. AD-integrated zones live inside ntds.dit instead.

items.name: Certificate Services database
items.description: AD CS database — issued certificates, pending requests, and CA logs. Only present on servers with the Certificate Services role installed.

items.name: DC health + replication (live)
items.description: Replication state and DC health. Run from the DC under investigation or a peer — unexpected replication partners or failures are worth chasing.

items.name: Physical Memory Dump
items.description: Full RAM capture — running processes, decrypted data, injected code. Use AVML or LiME.

items.name: Process List (ps auxef)
items.description: Process tree with full command lines, users, TTYs — the single most useful live artifact.

items.name: Network Connections (ss)
items.description: Active sockets with owning PID and process name. Preferred over deprecated netstat.

items.name: Open Files (lsof)
items.description: Every open FD: files, sockets, pipes, deleted-but-held files (common malware trick).

items.name: Loaded Kernel Modules
items.description: Kernel modules currently loaded. Compare against expected set; rootkits hide here.

items.name: Passive connection state (/proc/net)
items.description: Raw socket and conntrack tables straight from the kernel — fallback if ss/netstat aren't trusted, and richer than either for established and tracked connections.

items.name: /etc/os-release
items.description: Distro name, version ID, pretty name. First thing to collect to establish baseline.

items.name: /etc/issue and /etc/issue.net
items.description: Login banners — sometimes customised with hostname, warning text, distro branding.

items.name: /etc/hostname
items.description: System hostname.

items.name: Timezone (/etc/localtime, /etc/timezone)
items.description: Symlink to the zoneinfo file — essential for interpreting every timestamp you collect.

items.name: /etc/fstab
items.description: Mount-at-boot definitions — reveals external volumes, network shares, tmpfs layout.

items.name: Kernel version (uname -a)
items.description: Kernel, release, arch, build date. Pin the exact kernel for module compatibility.

items.name: Kernel Ring Buffer (dmesg)
items.description: Boot-time and runtime kernel messages — hardware events, OOM kills, module loads.

items.name: Live mount state (/proc/mounts)
items.description: Actual mounted filesystems right now — distinct from /etc/fstab which is only boot-time config. Reveals tmpfs, bind mounts, overlay/container mounts, and secret volumes.

items.name: /etc/passwd
items.description: User accounts, UIDs, home dirs, shells. Look for UID 0 duplicates and unexpected accounts.

items.name: /etc/shadow
items.description: Password hashes, last-change, expiry. Root-readable only.

items.name: /etc/group
items.description: Group membership — check who has sudo/wheel/docker/adm access.

items.name: /etc/sudoers and /etc/sudoers.d/
items.description: Sudo-permission rules. Drop-in files in sudoers.d are easy to miss.

items.name: Shell history (~/.bash_history)
items.description: Per-user command history. Also check .zsh_history, .ash_history, .python_history.

items.name: SSH keys and known_hosts (~/.ssh/)
items.description: authorized_keys (persistence!), known_hosts, private id_rsa/id_ed25519 — capture all.

items.name: Login records (wtmp/btmp/lastlog)
items.description: Binary logs of successful logins (wtmp), failed logins (btmp), last-login per user. Read with last, lastb, lastlog.

items.name: Other shell / app histories
items.description: Per-user histories for non-bash shells and common tools. Each file is plain text and survives across sessions — as valuable as bash_history and much more often overlooked.

items.name: sshd configuration
items.description: SSH daemon config — check PermitRootLogin, AuthorizedKeysFile overrides, Match blocks, and any drop-ins that weaken auth.

items.name: PAM configuration
items.description: Pluggable-auth modules — a malicious PAM module or a modified /etc/pam.d/sshd can silently bypass authentication. High-value persistence check.

items.name: /var/log/auth.log
items.description: SSH, sudo, PAM auth events on Debian-family distros.

items.name: /var/log/secure
items.description: SSH, sudo, PAM auth events on RHEL-family distros.

items.name: /var/log/syslog
items.description: General system messages on Debian-family.

items.name: /var/log/messages
items.description: General system messages on RHEL-family.

items.name: systemd journal
items.description: Binary structured logs — richer than text logs. Export with journalctl for offline read.

items.name: audit.log (auditd)
items.description: Kernel audit events — syscall tracing, file access, exec. Only present if auditd is installed and running.

items.name: Web server logs
items.description: Access and error logs — frequently record the initial entry point (brute force, webshell upload, SQLi). Don't forget the rotated archives in each directory.

items.name: fail2ban log
items.description: Banned IPs with trigger reasons and timestamps. Only present if fail2ban is installed.

items.name: Cron jobs
items.description: System and per-user cron — /var/spool/cron/ is frequently abused and easy to miss.

items.name: systemd unit files
items.description: Service definitions — including user units. Recently-modified .service files are suspicious.

items.name: /etc/rc.local
items.description: Legacy boot script. Not present by default on systemd distros but attackers sometimes recreate it.

items.name: Shell rc files
items.description: Runs on every login/shell start — a classic persistence hiding spot.

items.name: /etc/ld.so.preload
items.description: Libraries injected into every dynamically-linked binary. Should not exist on a clean system — presence is a rootkit red flag.

items.name: at jobs
items.description: One-shot scheduled jobs — a frequently-overlooked alternative to cron.

items.name: Kernel module auto-load config
items.description: Lists modules loaded at boot and their options. Rootkits can hide here as an extra entry with an innocuous name — diff against the distro defaults.

items.name: Kernel / initramfs images
items.description: Booted kernel and its early-userspace image. Sophisticated rootkits modify initramfs to re-infect even after a system re-install.

items.name: /etc/resolv.conf
items.description: DNS resolver config. Often symlinked to systemd-resolved's runtime file.

items.name: /etc/hosts
items.description: Static hostname→IP mappings. Poisoning here is a quick redirection trick.

items.name: Firewall rules (iptables / nft)
items.description: Active packet-filter rules. Capture both families in case the system uses one or the other.
items.command: iptables-save > iptables.txt && nft list ruleset > nft.txt

items.name: Routing and ARP (ip)
items.description: Routing table, ARP neighbours, interface list. Modern replacement for route/arp/ifconfig.
items.command: ip -br a > iface.txt && ip route > route.txt && ip neigh > arp.txt

items.name: netplan configuration
items.description: YAML network config used by Ubuntu since 18.04. Describes interfaces, bridges, VLANs, and the backend renderer (networkd vs NetworkManager).

items.name: NetworkManager connections
items.description: Per-connection keyfiles (Wi-Fi, VPN, wired) with SSIDs, auth settings, and sometimes pre-shared keys. Default networking stack on RHEL 8+ / Fedora.

items.name: Network scripts (ifcfg-*)
items.description: Legacy per-interface configuration on RHEL-family. Default on RHEL 7 / CentOS 7; still supported via a plugin on RHEL 8 / CentOS 8.

items.name: /etc/network/interfaces
items.description: Classic ifupdown config — default networking on Debian. May coexist with NetworkManager on desktop installs.

items.name: dpkg package list
items.description: All installed .deb packages with versions. Compare against a known-good baseline to spot planted packages.

items.name: APT install history
items.description: Timeline of every apt install/remove/upgrade with the invoking user.

items.name: rpm package list
items.description: All installed RPMs with versions, install time, vendor. rpm -Va checks for modified files against the package DB.

items.name: yum transaction log
items.description: Legacy yum install/remove history.

items.name: dnf transaction log
items.description: Modern dnf install/remove history — replaces yum.log.

items.name: Docker containers & logs
items.description: Per-container stdout/stderr log files and image layers. Pair with `docker ps -a` and `docker images` to map containers to IDs — payloads often live inside containers rather than on the host.
items.command: docker ps -a > containers.txt && docker images > images.txt

items.name: Snap packages
items.description: Parallel package system — snap lists, revisions, and per-snap data. Standard on Ubuntu.

items.name: Flatpak packages
items.description: Sandboxed application packages — common on Fedora and optional on RHEL/CentOS 8+. Apps run with their own library stack, so their content isn't visible in rpm -qa.

items.name: SUID / SGID binary snapshot
items.description: Finds every SUID/SGID-executable file on local filesystems. Compare against a known-good list to surface planted privilege-escalation binaries.

items.name: World-writable directories
items.description: Directories anyone can write to — attacker staging grounds beyond the usual /tmp, /var/tmp, /dev/shm.

items.name: Recently modified files (last 7 days)
items.description: Everything touched in the past week — catches fresh drops and config changes. Adjust the -mtime window to fit the incident timeline.

items.name: corosync.conf
items.description: Cluster membership, ring addresses, and quorum votes. Identical across every node via pmxcfs.

items.name: datacenter.cfg
items.description: Cluster-wide defaults — migration settings, HA policies, keyboard, language, console type.

items.name: storage.cfg
items.description: All storage pool definitions (Directory, NFS, CIFS, LVM, ZFS, Ceph, iSCSI, RBD). Tells you where VM images actually live.

items.name: user.cfg
items.description: PVE users, groups, and ACL entries (role assignments).

items.name: domains.cfg
items.description: Authentication realms — PAM, PVE, LDAP, Active Directory, OpenID Connect.

items.name: PVE realm password hashes
items.description: Password hashes for users in the PVE realm (separate from PAM / Linux shadow). Root-readable only.

items.name: PVE JWT signing keys
items.description: Keys used to sign session tickets. Possession means being able to forge valid PVE sessions.

items.name: 2FA configuration
items.description: Per-user TOTP and webauthn secrets.

items.name: API token store
items.description: All API tokens with their associated users, expiry, and privsep flags. Tokens are a common persistence mechanism.

items.name: VM configs (qemu-server)
items.description: Per-VM config — CPU, memory, attached disks, network bridges, cloud-init snippets, boot order.

items.name: Container configs (LXC)
items.description: Per-LXC config — template, rootfs, mount points, unprivileged flag, features (nesting, keyctl).

items.name: Proxmox firewall rules
items.description: PVE firewall rule files at cluster, node, and per-VM/CT scopes. Compiled into nftables by pve-firewall.

items.name: pveproxy access log
items.description: Every web UI and API request with source IP, method, URL, and user. First stop for unauthorised access or brute-force.

items.name: Task archive
items.description: Every VM start/stop/backup/restore/migrate leaves a record here with the invoking user and full command output.

items.name: Template download log
items.description: pveam (template manager) download and update history.

items.name: pve-firewall log
items.description: pve-firewall daemon events — reload errors, ruleset compile output.

items.name: pvedaemon / pveproxy / pvestatd events
items.description: Debian daemon.log — includes PVE internal service events and status updates alongside system daemons.

items.name: VM and container list
items.description: Current VM + container inventory with status and resource usage.
items.command: qm list > vms.txt && pct list > cts.txt

items.name: Cluster status
items.description: Cluster members, quorum state, expected votes.
items.command: pvecm status > cluster.txt && pvecm nodes > nodes.txt

items.name: Full API resource snapshot
items.description: Snapshot of every cluster resource — VMs, CTs, storages, pools — via the authenticated API. Richer than qm/pct.

items.name: HA state
items.description: High-availability service + fence state. Only meaningful if HA is configured.

items.name: Backup configuration
items.description: Default vzdump settings and the cluster-wide backup schedule.

items.name: Backup archives
items.path: (from storage.cfg — typically /var/lib/vz/dump/ or a PBS target)
items.description: .vma, .vma.gz, .tar.zst archives. Older backups often contain evidence since deleted from live VMs.

items.name: ZFS pool and dataset state
items.description: Pool health, dataset list (including per-VM zvols), and full operation history — covers every ZFS action since pool creation.
items.command: zpool status -v > zpool.txt && zpool history > zhistory.txt && zfs list -t all > zfs.txt

items.name: LVM state
items.description: Volume groups, PVs, and LVs — including lvm-thin volumes backing VM disks.
items.command: pvs > pvs.txt && vgs > vgs.txt && lvs -a > lvs.txt

items.name: Ceph configuration (if used)
items.description: Ceph cluster config and keyrings as seen from PVE. Only present on clusters with integrated Ceph.

items.name: Ceph live state (if used)
items.description: Cluster health, OSD map, pool usage. Run from any PVE node with Ceph.
items.command: ceph -s > ceph.txt && ceph osd tree >> ceph.txt

items.name: esx.conf
items.description: Main host configuration — networking, NTP, services, boot-time settings. Often the first artifact to examine.

items.name: hostd config
items.description: Management agent (hostd) configuration — listeners, logging levels, certificate paths.

items.name: Local accounts (passwd/shadow)
items.description: Local root and ESXi shell accounts. Check for additions beyond root/dcui/nobody/vpxuser.

items.name: Host SSL certificates
items.description: Host TLS certificate + key, vpxuser cert (for vCenter), CA bundle.

items.name: System version + identity
items.description: ESXi build, product, UUID, hostname — pins the exact patch level the host was on.
items.command: esxcli system version get > version.txt && esxcli system hostname get >> version.txt && esxcli system uuid get >> version.txt

items.name: .vmx files (VM configs)
items.description: Per-VM config — vCPU, memory, NIC MACs, datastore paths of each disk, attached ISOs, nvram path.

items.name: VM disks (.vmdk + -flat.vmdk)
items.description: The descriptor (.vmdk) is tiny text; the -flat.vmdk holds actual data. For snapshots the deltas live in -delta.vmdk files.

items.name: Per-VM log (vmware.log)
items.description: Per-VM events — power operations, snapshot operations, VMX errors, guest IP discovery.

items.name: Suspend / snapshot memory (.vmss, .vmsn)
items.description: Saved-state memory dumps — suspended VMs and memory-included snapshots. Equivalent to a RAM capture of the guest at that point.

items.name: Firewall ruleset XMLs
items.description: service.xml plus any custom ruleset XMLs. Each describes which ports a service opens.

items.name: Active firewall rulesets
items.description: Which rulesets are currently enabled and the allowed IP ranges per ruleset.
items.command: esxcli network firewall ruleset list > fw.txt && esxcli network firewall ruleset allowedip list >> fw.txt

items.name: VM auto-start policy
items.description: Which VMs start automatically at host boot and in what order.

items.name: VM inventory file
items.description: Authoritative list of VMs registered with this host — including paths to their .vmx files.

items.name: hostd.log
items.description: Management agent log — API calls, authentication events, VM lifecycle requests.

items.name: vmkernel.log + vmkwarning.log + vmksummary.log
items.description: Hypervisor kernel messages — vmkernel is verbose; vmkwarning is filtered warnings; vmksummary is daily summaries.

items.name: auth.log
items.description: SSH and ESXi shell authentication events.

items.name: shell.log
items.description: Every command typed at the ESXi shell. High-value for post-compromise analysis.

items.name: vpxa.log (vCenter agent, if managed)
items.description: Communication with vCenter. Only present on hosts managed by vCenter.

items.name: syslog.log
items.description: General ESXi syslog — a catch-all that's separate from the per-component logs above.

items.name: Registered VMs
items.description: Every VM registered with hostd — World IDs, display names, and the .vmx path.

items.name: Running VMs (worlds)
items.description: Currently-executing VMs with their World IDs, Config File paths, and VMX Cartel IDs.

items.name: Active network connections
items.description: Host-kernel-tracked connections — ESXi's equivalent of netstat.
items.command: esxcli network ip connection list > netconns.txt

items.name: Middleware database
items.description: SQLite master config — users, groups, shares (SMB/NFS/iSCSI), replication, cloud-sync, API keys, VMs, apps, alerts, certificates. The single most-important file on a TrueNAS.

items.name: Password-encryption secret
items.description: Secret key used to encrypt sensitive fields (passwords, API keys) inside freenas-v1.db. Without it those fields cannot be decrypted.

items.name: Pool status + history
items.description: Pool health plus full operation history (creates, imports, scrubs, replacements) — goes back to pool creation.
items.command: zpool status -v > zpool.txt && zpool history > zhistory.txt

items.name: Datasets and snapshots
items.description: Complete list of datasets, volumes, and snapshots with sizes and creation dates.

items.name: Boot environments
items.description: Boot environments — each upgrade/rollback is a snapshot. beadm on CORE, zectl on SCALE.
items.command: beadm list > be.txt 2>/dev/null || zectl list > be.txt

items.name: Samba config
items.description: Generated Samba config — shares, auth backend, VFS modules. UI edits round-trip through the middleware DB.

items.name: NFS exports
items.description: Active NFS export list — generated from the middleware DB at config apply time.

items.name: iSCSI targets
items.description: All iSCSI targets, portals, initiator groups, and extents live in freenas-v1.db; /etc/ctl.conf (CORE) or /etc/scst.conf (SCALE) are generated from it.

items.name: FreeBSD rc.conf (CORE)
items.description: FreeBSD service + interface config. On CORE, network interfaces, routing, and enabled services live here.

items.name: Network config (SCALE)
items.description: On SCALE, networking is driven by the middleware and rendered via systemd-networkd. Check both paths for the active config.

items.name: middlewared log
items.description: Middleware daemon log — every UI action, API call, and internal event routes through here. High-value for forensics.

items.name: messages (CORE)
items.description: FreeBSD general syslog — kernel and daemon events.

items.name: syslog (SCALE)
items.description: Debian-style syslog on SCALE.

items.name: auth.log
items.description: SSH + sudo + local-auth events. Same location on both CORE and SCALE.

items.name: Samba logs
items.description: SMB session events — failed auth, share connects. Each connecting client gets a per-client log file.

items.name: iocage jails (CORE)
items.description: Each FreeBSD jail has its own rootfs, config, and fstab here. Treat each jail as a nested host worth its own triage pass.

items.name: Apps (SCALE — k3s)
items.description: SCALE apps run on k3s. Charts, per-app PV data, and container state live here. Pair with a k3s kubectl inventory snapshot.

items.name: Virtual machines
items.path: (stored in the middleware DB; disks on ZFS volumes)
items.description: VM definitions live in freenas-v1.db; disks are zvols named `<pool>/<vm-name>-<suffix>`. CORE uses bhyve; SCALE uses KVM/libvirt.

items.name: SSH daemon config
items.description: sshd_config and host keys. Middleware regenerates sshd_config on changes, so check for unexpected overrides.

items.name: SMART disk data
items.description: Persisted SMART history per disk. Pair with a live smartctl -a snapshot for each disk.
items.command: for d in $(lsblk -ndo NAME 2>/dev/null); do smartctl -a /dev/$d > smart-$d.txt; done

items.name: config.boot (active config)
items.description: The active, persisted VyOS configuration in its native structured format — firewall rules, NAT, VPN, interfaces, users, everything.

items.name: Config archive
items.description: Every commit auto-saves a timestamped copy here. Build a commit-by-commit timeline of how the box drifted from a known-good baseline.

items.name: User scripts
items.description: Custom scripts referenced by VyOS task schedulers, commit hooks, or policy-based routing. Common persistence spot for dropped scripts.

items.name: WireGuard keys
items.description: Per-interface private keys and peer public keys, if WireGuard tunnels are configured.

items.name: External auth configs
items.description: RADIUS, TACACS, LDAP configuration files referenced from config.boot.

items.name: /etc/passwd
items.description: Regenerated from config.boot on commit. Check for accounts that exist here but not in config.boot (or vice-versa).

items.name: /etc/shadow
items.description: Password hashes. Same caveat as /etc/passwd — hashes for config-defined users are derived on commit.

items.name: SSH authorized_keys
items.description: VyOS lets you paste keys into config, and they materialise here. Direct edits bypass config and are a hiding spot.

items.name: frr.conf (generated)
items.description: FRRouting config, generated from config.boot. Useful as a sanity check that the routing subsystem matches the declared config.

items.name: FRR daemon logs
items.description: Per-protocol FRR logs (bgpd, ospfd, zebra). Session flaps and convergence events land here.

items.name: auth.log
items.description: SSH and login auth events — standard Debian-style log under VyOS.

items.name: messages / syslog
items.description: General system logs. On VyOS both paths may exist — capture whichever is present.

items.name: Running config (operational)
items.description: The committed active config rendered as set-commands. Easier to diff against a known-good baseline than config.boot.

items.name: Firewall counters + state
items.description: Rule-by-rule packet/byte counters and dynamic groups.

items.name: NAT rules + counters
items.description: Source-NAT and destination-NAT rule evaluation — useful for mapping internal ↔ external flows.
items.command: show nat source rules > snat.txt && show nat destination rules > dnat.txt

items.name: Routing tables (all protocols)
items.description: Active routing decisions — static, BGP, OSPF, RIP. Also shows route-leaks between VRFs.
items.command: show ip route > route.txt && show ip bgp summary >> route.txt && show ip ospf neighbor >> route.txt

items.name: config.xml
items.description: The entire firewall configuration as a single XML file — firewall rules, NAT, VPN, CAs, certificates, users, packages, DHCP, DNS. Single source of truth.

items.name: Config backup archive
items.description: Every config change creates a new timestamped backup file here. Full commit-by-commit history of how the firewall evolved.

items.name: RRD traffic databases
items.description: Per-interface traffic and state-table histograms — throughput, states, queue data over weeks/months.

items.name: Active pf rules
items.description: Compiled ruleset — may differ from config.xml if a package has injected anchors.

items.name: Active NAT rules
items.description: Source + destination NAT rules currently in effect.

items.name: State table
items.description: Every currently-tracked connection — src, dst, protocol, state, bytes. Equivalent to netstat but evaluated by pf.

items.name: filter.log (firewall log)
items.description: Circular binary log (clog format) of pf pass/block events. Read with `clog` or via the pfSense UI log viewer.

items.name: system.log
items.description: General system events — also a clog circular log on pfSense.

items.name: auth.log
items.description: SSH and web UI authentication events.

items.name: dhcpd.log
items.description: DHCP server lease events. Maps MAC addresses to IPs over time.

items.name: openvpn.log
items.description: OpenVPN server and client session events — who connected, from where, which cert.

items.name: ipsec.log
items.description: IPsec daemon events — phase 1/2 negotiation, rekeys, failed auths.

items.name: IDS logs (Snort/Suricata)
items.description: Only present if the Snort or Suricata package is installed. Alerts plus full event logs per interface.

items.name: Generated IPsec config
items.description: strongSwan config and PSKs rendered from config.xml at apply time.

items.name: Generated OpenVPN configs
items.description: Per-server / per-client OpenVPN configuration directories with cert and key material.

items.name: WireGuard configuration (if installed)
items.description: WireGuard is a package on pfSense 2.5/2.6 and native from 2.7. Keys end up in config.xml and/or this directory.

items.name: FreeBSD password DB
items.description: The system-level FreeBSD account database. pfSense web-UI users live in config.xml; system-level users (root, admin) are here.

items.name: Web UI users (in config.xml)
items.description: Web UI users, groups, and their hashed passwords live in config.xml. Attackers may add a new admin user — diff against a clean baseline.
